languages.text: "Они не офицеры, а командиры. Есть разница. Дядя, в смысле полковник, уже больше десятка сезонов полк возглавляет. Никого честнее и сильнее дядьки на Заркове нету. Я сам видал как он отход раненых прикрывал. На него три присоски с булавы прицепились, а он на них даже не отвлекся, пока раненого до Клети не донес. Присоски потом сами отвалились и сдохли, говорят..."

"They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — I mean, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I once saw him covering a retreat for the wounded. Three suckers from a mace latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."
languages.original_text: "Они не офицеры, а командиры. Есть разница. Дядя, в смысле полковник, уже больше десятка сезонов полк возглавляет. Никого честнее и сильнее дядьки на Заркове нету. Присоски потом сами отвалились и сдохли, говорят..."

traits.locale_text: "They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — well, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I saw him covering a a retreat for the wounded. Three mace-suckers latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."

"They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — I mean, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I once saw him covering a retreat for the wounded. Three suckers from a mace latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."

"They're commanders. There's a difference. Colonel Batoshski has been in charge of the regiment for a decade or more now, after a series of training missions where he defeated all the other contenders. And without killing hardly anyone, if you can believe it! He spared their lives. But that's our Izdrig all over — a father to everyone. Someone who takes care of the regiment."

You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I saw him covering a a retreat for the wounded. "They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — I mean, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I once saw him covering a retreat for the wounded. Three suckers from a mace latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."

languages.text: 「彼らは指揮官です。違いがあります。バトシュスキー大佐は、訓練任務の連続で他の候補者をすべて打ち負かした後、10年以上もこの連隊を指揮してきました。信じられないかもしれませんが、ほとんど人を殺さずに。彼らを助けてあげました。でも、それが私たちのイズドリーグです。みんなを父親のように見ている。連隊を世話している人です」
languages.original_text: "They're commanders. There's a difference. Colonel Batoshski has been in charge of the regiment for a decade or more now, after a series of training missions where he defeated all the other contenders. And without killing hardly anyone, if you can believe it! He spared their lives. But that's our Izdrig all over — a father to everyone. Someone who takes care of the regiment."

languages.text: "Onlar komutan, subay değil. Arada fark var. Amcam... Yani Albay, on mevsimden fazladır alayın başında. Zarkov'da ondan daha dürüst ya da daha çetin birini bulamazsınız. Bir keresinde geri çekilen yaralıları korurken görmüştüm onu. Bir gürz yaratığından fırlayan üç sülük ona yapışmıştı, o ise yaralı askeri Kafes'e ulaştırana kadar gözünü bile kırpmadı. Dediklerine göre sülükler sonradan kuruyup ölmüş."
languages.original_text: "They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — I mean, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I once saw him covering a retreat for the wounded. Three suckers from a mace latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."

traits.locale_text: "Onlar komutan, subay değil. Arada fark var. Amcam... Yani Albay, on mevsimden fazladır alayın başında. Zarkov'da ondan daha dürüst ya da daha çetin birini bulamazsınız. Bir keresinde geri çekilen yaralıları korurken görmüştüm onu. Bir gürz yaratığından fırlayan üç sülük ona yapışmıştı, o ise yaralı askeri Kafes'e ulaştırana kadar gözünü bile kırpmadı. Dediklerine göre sülükler sonradan kuruyup ölmüş."

languages.text: “他们是指挥官，不是什么军官。这不一样。我叔叔——我是说，上校——掌管这个军团已经十多年了。在扎尔科夫，再也找不到比他更正派、更硬气的人了。我亲眼见过他掩护伤员撤退。一根藤锤上的三个吸盘咬在他身上，可他连眼皮都没眨一下，将那伤兵送回了牢笼。他们说，那些吸盘后来就枯死在他身上了。”
languages.original_text: "They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — I mean, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I once saw him covering a retreat for the wounded. Three suckers from a mace latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."

traits.locale_text: “他们是指挥官，不是什么军官。这不一样。我叔叔——我是说，上校——掌管这个军团已经十多年了。在扎尔科夫，再也找不到比他更正派、更硬气的人了。我亲眼见过他掩护伤员撤退。一根藤锤上的三个吸盘咬在他身上，可他连眼皮都没眨一下，将那伤兵送回了牢笼。他们说，那些吸盘后来就枯死在他身上了。”

languages.text: "Sie sind Kommandeure, keine Offiziere. Das ist was anderes. Mein Onkel – also der Colonel – war zehn Zyklen lang für das Regiment verantwortlich. Ihr werdet auf Zarkov niemanden finden, der anständiger oder härter ist als er. Einmal hat er einem Verwundeten den Rückzug ermöglicht. Drei Blüten haben nach ihm geschnappt, aber er hat nicht mal gezwinkert, bis der verletzte Soldat im Käfig war. Es heißt die Blüten sind einfach vertrocknet und dann gestorben."
languages.original_text: "They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — I mean, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I once saw him covering a retreat for the wounded. Three suckers from a mace latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."

traits.locale_text: "Sie sind Kommandeure, keine Offiziere. Das ist was anderes. Mein Onkel – also der Colonel – war zehn Zyklen lang für das Regiment verantwortlich. Ihr werdet auf Zarkov niemanden finden, der anständiger oder härter ist als er. Einmal hat er einem Verwundeten den Rückzug ermöglicht. Drei Blüten haben nach ihm geschnappt, aber er hat nicht mal gezwinkert, bis der verletzte Soldat im Käfig war. Es heißt die Blüten sind einfach vertrocknet und dann gestorben."

languages.text: « Pas tout à fait. Ce sont des commandants, pas des officiers. Voilà plus de dix saisons que le régiment est dirigé par mon oncle... le colonel, je veux dire. Vous ne trouverez personne sur Zarkov de plus fiable ou de plus coriace que lui. Un jour, je l'ai vu protéger des soldats blessés pendant qu'ils battaient en retraite. Trois ventouses d'une masse se sont accrochées à lui, mais il a poursuivi sa route comme si de rien n'était jusqu'à ce que tout le monde soit en lieu sûr. Il paraît que les ventouses sont tombées toutes seules ensuite, complètement desséchées. »
languages.original_text: "They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — I mean, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I once saw him covering a retreat for the wounded. Three suckers from a mace latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."

traits.locale_text: « Pas tout à fait. Ce sont des commandants, pas des officiers. Voilà plus de dix saisons que le régiment est dirigé par mon oncle... le colonel, je veux dire. Vous ne trouverez personne sur Zarkov de plus fiable ou de plus coriace que lui. Un jour, je l'ai vu protéger des soldats blessés pendant qu'ils battaient en retraite. Trois ventouses d'une masse se sont accrochées à lui, mais il a poursuivi sa route comme si de rien n'était jusqu'à ce que tout le monde soit en lieu sûr. Il paraît que les ventouses sont tombées toutes seules ensuite, complètement desséchées. »

languages.text: «Son comandantes, no oficiales. Esa es la diferencia. Mi tío... quiero decir, el coronel, lleva más de diez estaciones al mando del regimiento. No hay nadie más decente ni duro que él en Zarkov. Una vez, lo vi cubriendo la retirada de unos heridos. Se le pegaron tres ventosas de una maza, pero ni parpadeó hasta que el último herido llegó a la Jaula. Dicen que, después, las ventosas se marchitaron».
languages.original_text: "They're commanders not officers. There's a difference. My uncle — I mean, the colonel — has been in charge of the regiment for over ten seasons. You won't find anyone on Zarkov who is more decent or tougher than him. I once saw him covering a retreat for the wounded. Three suckers from a mace latched onto him, but he didn't even blink until he got the wounded soldier to the Cage. They say the suckers just shrivelled up and died later."
